items.title: Berkshire Hathaway Has Had a Tough Summer. Will It Get a Buffett Swansong Boost?
items.description: Warren Buffett isn’t used to lagging behind the market.  The billionaire’s  Berkshire Hathaway  investment group has firmly outpaced the  over the past five years, and has delivered staggering returns since he began converting it from a textile company into an acquisition machine in the mid-1960s.  Buffett, who turns 95 later this month, announced his effective retirement last spring by naming Greg Abel, a longtime Berkshire colleague, as his successor.

items.title: Australia Launch & AMEX Deal: Will TOST's Recipe Fuel its Growth Engine?
items.description: Toast projects 29% growth in 2025 as international expansion and a new AMEX partnership fuel momentum in restaurant tech.

items.title: AmEx Up 24.3% in a Year: But Is the Price Target Enough of a Perk?
items.description: AXP gains 24.3% in a year, but with shares near Wall Street's target, is the growth runway losing momentum?

items.title: American Express Adds to its Expansive Global Sports and Entertainment Portfolio with New Partnerships with Hard Rock Stadium, the Formula 1® Crypto.com Miami Grand Prix and the Miami Dolphins
items.description: NEW YORK, August 20, 2025--American Express will now offer even more access and perks for Card Members in South Florida as the new Official Payments Partner of Hard Rock Stadium, the FORMULA 1® CRYPTO.COM MIAMI GRAND PRIX and the Miami Dolphins. The new, multi-year partnership will provide eligible Amex® Card Members with access to Amex Presale Tickets™, special fan experiences, exclusive lounges and more.
